title: Allocate the credit line to Payment Portfolios
content:
Use this endpoint to allocate the credit line of a client to one or more Payment Portfolios. ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/payment_portfolio/credit_line/update/ **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field{30%}|Data Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type.
Allowed format: `application/json`. | ``` **Parameters** ``` xtable |Field{30%}|Data Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |--- |--- |--- | |allocation_details{Required}|object[]|The details of credit line allocation.

Max size: 10.| #|payment_portfolio_id{Required}|string|The ID of the Advanced Payment Portfolio.

To obtain a list of available Payment Portfolio IDs, use [/payment_portfolio/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1839151931301954) and filter for Payment Portfolios where the `payment_portfolio_type` is `SHARED`.| #|allocation_percentage{Required}|number|The percentage of credit line that you want to allocate to the Payment Portfolio, kept to four decimal places.
Value range: 0-1.

Note:

  • The percentage you allocate to each Payment Portfolio must be greater than its used percentage. To confirm the used credit line percentage for a Payment Portfolio, check the `min_allocation_percentage` returned from [/payment_portfolio/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=18391519313019545).
  • If your total credit line changes later, the credit line for each Portfolio will change based on the set percentage.
  • Each Portfolio's available credit line is automatically restored when outstanding bills are settled. You won't need to manually reallocate the amount.
| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location --request POST 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/payment_portfolio/credit_line/update/' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "allocation_details": [ { "payment_portfolio_id": "{{payment_portfolio_id}}", "allocation_percentage": {{allocation_percentage}} }, { "payment_portfolio_id": "{{payment_portfolio_id}}", "allocation_percentage": {{allocation_percentage}} } ] }' (/code) ``` ## Response ``` xtable |Field{30%}|Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix-Return Code](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737172488964097). | |message|string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id |string|The log ID of a request, which uniquely identifies the request. | |data |object|Returned data.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": {} } (/code) ```
